description.en-US: Aquinas's earliest philosophical treatise (c. 1252) on the metaphysics of being — distinguishing essence and existence, and showing how this distinction underwrites the contingency of creatures and the unique necessity of God.
description.la: Tractatus philosophicus Thomae primus (c. 1252) de metaphysica entis — essentiam et esse distinguens, et hanc distinctionem contingentiam creaturarum et unicam Dei necessitatem fundare ostendens.

sources.description: English translation by Armand Maurer (PIMS, Toronto, 1949). Mirrored from aquinas.cc.
Latin text from the Leonine / Marietti editions (public domain), mirrored from aquinas.cc.

title.en-US: Chapter 1 — On the meaning of the terms ‘being’ and ‘essence’
Chapter 2 — On the essences of composite substances
Chapter 3 — How essence is related to genus, species, and difference
Chapter 4 — How essences exist in separated substances
Chapter 5 — How essence is found in various kinds of things
Chapter 6 — How there are essences in accidents
